channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Australian candidate challenges Nigel Farage in UK by-election, highlighting cross-border policy influences and Trump's World Cup remarks
item.description: The article explores the political landscape surrounding an Australian candidate, Daniel Pocock, running in a UK by-election against Nigel Farage. Pocock's campaign highlights how UK policies have inc...

item.title: World Karate Federation Supports Interim Leadership Amid Disciplinary Proceedings Against Sonny Pillay's Executive
item.description: The World Karate Federation (WKF) has confirmed that Karate South Africa (KSA) remains a full member of the global body despite the suspension of its national executive led by long-serving president S...

item.title: Guide to Checking and Settling NCAP Traffic Violations via eGovPH App
item.description: The eGovPH Super App now allows motorists in the Philippines to access and monitor traffic violations under the No Contact Apprehension Policy (NCAP), launched by the MMDA and DICT. Users can register...

item.title: Alberta Separatist Lawyer Faces Legal Disputes Over Indigenous Law Cases
item.description: Jeffrey Rath, a prominent Alberta separatist lawyer known for his work with First Nations communities, is facing significant legal challenges. A court has imposed an asset freeze on Rath and his firm,...

item.title: Spain Coach De La Fuente Celebrates Team's Growth and World Cup Victory
item.description: Spain's coach, Luis de La Fuente, expressed pride in his team after they clinched their second World Cup title. He highlighted the players' exceptional talent and their role as a source of pride for S...

item.title: Blondie's 1977 UK Tour Photos Capture Punk Era Vibes
item.description: In May 1977, the punk band Blondie arrived in the UK for their first-ever British tour, supporting Television. Music photographer Ian Dickson captured candid street portraits of the band during a prom...

item.title: Sonam Wangchuk says he will end hunger strike if government accepts accountability or MPs assure Parliament discussion
item.description: Activist Sonam Wangchuk, who has been on an indefinite hunger strike for 23 days, said he would end his fast on July 20 if specific conditions related to accountability in the education sector were me...

item.title: Technical access issue prevents reading news article
item.description: The article content indicates a technical barrier preventing access to the news piece. News Corp Australia employs software to manage crawler bot traffic, resulting in a block for non-bot users. Reade...

item.title: First Child of Sitting US Vice President Born in 156 Years
item.description: US Vice President JD Vance na wife Usha have welcomed their fourth child, a baby boy named Alec Neel Vance, marking a historic moment as the first child born to a sitting US vice president in 156 year...

item.title: Trump's Gangster Tactics and the Gordie Howe Bridge Dispute
item.description: The article explores allegations that former U.S. President Donald Trump employed gangster-like tactics in his foreign policy, using the contentious dispute over the Gordie Howe Bridge between Canada ...

item.title: DPWH Chief Dizon Commits to Cooperate in 2019 SEA Games Spending Probe
item.description: Department of Public Works and Highways (DPWH) Secretary Vince Dizon has affirmed his willingness to cooperate with the National Bureau of Investigation (NBI) in the probe into alleged irregularities ...

item.title: VDM Calls for Investigation of Budget Office and Arrest of DG Over PFIPC Budget Allegations
item.description: Social media activist Martins Vincent Otse, popularly known as VeryDarkMan (VDM), has reacted to the arrest of Adeniyi Adeyemi, the controversial Director-General of the Presidential Intervention Prom...

item.title: Kimi Antonelli extends F1 title lead with Belgian GP victory
item.description: Kimi Antonelli secured a dominant victory at the 2026 Belgian Grand Prix, extending his Formula One championship lead. The 19-year-old Mercedes rookie started from pole position and overcame challenge...

item.title: US President Trump Joins Spain in World Cup Trophy Presentation Amid Mixed Reactions
item.description: Donald Trump, the US president, attended the FIFA World Cup final between Spain and Argentina at MetLife Stadium in New Jersey. The match concluded with Spain winning 1-0 after extra-time, thanks to F...

item.title: FDA Revises Cyclospora Test Results for Taylor Farms Lettuce
item.description: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) has clarified that a previous positive cyclospora test result from Taylor Farms' shredded iceberg lettuce was a false positive. Initial findings suggested t...

item.title: Senegal President Bassirou Faye Takes Over as New ECOWAS Chairman
item.description: President Bassirou Faye of Senegal has emerged as the new Chairman of the Economic Community of West African States (ECOWAS), marking a fresh leadership phase for the regional bloc. According to a sta...

item.title: Protest against Ken-Betwa river linking project intensifies over land disputes
item.description: The Ken-Betwa Link Project, India's first major river interlinking initiative, has sparked widespread protests in Madhya Pradesh due to alleged irregularities in land acquisition and compensation for ...

item.title: Health Impacts of Night Shifts and Biphasic Sleep Solutions
item.description: The article explores the health risks associated with night shifts, including increased risks of dementia, heart disease, and cancer, linked to disrupted sleep patterns and the body's circadian rhythm...

item.title: Spain clinches World Cup victory amid Madrid celebrations
item.description: In Madrid, fans braved sweltering temperatures and the sluggish pace of a World Cup final to celebrate Spain's 1-0 win over Argentina. With temperatures nearing 32°C at 9pm, supporters filled bars, pa...

item.title: Councillor criticises City of Cape Town's lack of progress on informal settlements
item.description: Councillor Bongani Ngcombolo has expressed deep concern over the City of Cape Town's failure to address the dire conditions in informal settlements, particularly after a fatal shack fire in Kanana. Th...
